channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La búsqueda del hidrógeno limpio bajo tierra
item.description: El sueño del hidrógeno limpio ha seducido a los expertos en energía durante años, pero producirlo ha sido difícil. Muchas empresas emergentes creen que la respuesta podría estar bajo nuestros pies.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Ian Willms

media:content.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A drilling site operated by Vema Hydrogen in Thetford Mines, Quebec.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 19. 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Clubs
item.description: Do you belong to any? If you could start a club around anything, what would it be, and why?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Emon Hassan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chris Kobara practices writing his name in a cursive writing club at the Urban Assembly Early College High School of Emergency Medicine in Manhattan.

item.title: Interest in N.Y.C. Pre-K Programs Is Flat, Despite Mamdani’s Outreach
item.description: Expanding child care is a pillar of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s affordability agenda. Newly released application numbers may raise questions about strategy and demand.
item.dc:creator: Eliza Shapiro and Matthew Haag

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani got budget help from Gov. Kathy Hochul this year to fund pre-K expansion.

item.title: Motherhood Changed Ilana Glazer. A Reporter Knew What That Felt Like.
item.description: Sometimes a reporter and subject can converse on a common ground.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

media:content.media:credit: Aundre Larrow for The New York Times

item.title: Deal Ends Long Island Rail Road Strike
item.description: The Long Island Rail Road strike ended after transit officials and union representatives reached a deal. Tuesday’s commute was expected to be disrupted even as regular train service resumed.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper, Michael Anthony Adams and Jiwoong Hong

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: Jon Stewart Asks What ‘Daddy’ Brought Back From China
item.description: “He was gone for a couple of days in China. We were scared,” Stewart said of President Trump on Monday’s “The Daily Show.” “But now he’s home, and I’m sure he brought us all the goodies.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: ‘El señor de las moscas’ ya no nos asusta
item.description: La brutalidad contemporánea hace que la novela de William Golding ya no resulte tan perturbadora como alguna vez lo fue.
item.dc:creator: Junot Díaz

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración por The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: altruistic
item.description: This word has appeared in 26 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Startled Horse Topples a Carriage in Central Park, Injuring Its Driver
item.description: New York City’s horse-drawn carriages have been the subject of a long-running political debate over animal welfare, public safety and jobs.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:credit: Seth Wenig/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A horse-dawn carriage taking passengers through Central Park in New York last year.

item.title: Last-Minute Donation Could Upend G.O.P. Primary for Texas Attorney General
item.description: A single donor’s contribution of $2.75 million could help propel Representative Chip Roy in his clash with a self-funded state senator, Mayes Middleton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

item.title: At Cannes, the Movies Are Divisive and the Arguments Heated
item.description: Without strong front-runners for the Palme d’Or, every movie is getting mixed reactions, especially the sci-fi action film “Hope.”
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

media:content.media:credit: Blanca Cruz/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Michael Fassbender and Alicia Vikander at the Cannes news conference for their polarizing film, “Hope.”

item.title: Trump’s Portrait Pops Up on Rickshaws in India to Mark America’s 250th
item.description: The president is popping up on the back of the iconic three-wheeler to kick off America’s 250th anniversary celebrations in India.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli

media:content.media:credit: Anindito Mukherjee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Md. Tayemkhan, a rickshaw driver, waited for hours near the U.S. Embassy in New Delhi to get a banner featuring President Donald Trump put on the back of his vehicle. He said he was paid about $16.

item.title: Oil Slick Reaches a Pristine Persian Gulf Island in Iran
item.description: Videos show birds, turtles and crabs trapped inside mounds of tar around Shidvar island, a protected wildlife sanctuary with turquoise waters and white sand beaches.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi, Erika Solomon and Devon Lum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: @ehsanjalali2000, via Instagram
media:content.media:description: A video verified by The Times shows oil coating the shoreline of Shidvar Island as smoke rises from the Lavan refinery, seen in the distance.

item.description: The campaign, organized by the U.S. Embassy in India, features President Trump on some auto rickshaws and the Statue of Liberty on others.

item.title: Corrections: May 19, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, May 19, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Spotlight Left Greenland, but U.S. Interest Hasn’t Wavered
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, May 19, 2026.

item.title: Trump Is Said to Have Proposed a New Helipad at the White House
item.description: The exhaust from helicopters’ engines has been known to occasionally scorch the grass on their landing zones.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Minho Kim

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump walking to board Marine One on the South Lawn of the White House earlier this month.

item.title: A Fearful Phone Call and Frantic Search Preceded San Diego Mosque Shooting
item.description: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police officers raced around San Diego before converging at the mosque.

item.title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
item.description: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow and Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson of the Supreme Court talks with Judge Richard Gergel of the U.S. District Court in South Carolina during a fireside chat hosted by the American Law Institute in Washington on Monday.

item.title: Nauru Hopes You Will Call It by a New Name
item.description: Tiny Nauru could soon be called Naoero.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 19, 2026
item.description: Cheers to you, Brad Wiegmann.
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Miller for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: This puzzle will warm you right up.

item.title: Colorado Supreme Court Orders Hospital to Restart Care for Transgender Minors
item.description: Children’s Hospital Colorado had halted hormone therapy and puberty blockers for transgender youths amid threats from the Trump administration to withhold funding from institutions that provide it.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Chet Strange/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Ralph L. Carr Colorado Judicial Center, home of the Colorado Supreme Court in Denver.

item.title: Top Treasury Lawyer Resigns After Creation of ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’
item.description: Brian Morrissey, the department’s general counsel, stepped down hours after the Trump administration announced the $1.8 billion fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Treasury Department’s general counsel, Brian Morrissey, resigned on Monday, three people familiar with the move said.

item.title: Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
item.description: The Trump administration argues that an “emergency refugee situation” in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at a cost of some $100 million.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and his aides have claimed that white South Africans face racial persecution, an assertion strongly disputed by officials in that country.

item.title: Judge Bars ICE From Making Immigration Arrests at Courts in New York
item.description: The Trump administration’s practice of detaining migrants at federal courthouses had stirred outrage.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mask-wearing federal agents had become a regular presence in the hallways of 26 Federal Plaza in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Comes Amid Rising Reports of Islamophobia in the U.S.
item.description: The Council on American-Islamic Relations said complaints about bias against Muslims are at levels unseen in decades.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:credit: Mike Blake/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers swarmed to the scene of a shooting at the Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday.

item.title: EE. UU. aumenta la presión sobre Cuba y sanciona a altos funcionarios
item.description: La medida supone una importante escalada en la campaña del gobierno de Trump para obligar al gobierno comunista a revisar su sistema económico y político.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:credit: Norlys Perez/Reuters
media:content.media:description: La capital cubana esta semana

item.title: ‘The Emporium’ Review: Thornton Wilder Doesn’t Make the Sale
item.description: This newly discovered play by Wilder is part picaresque, part fable, featuring a Midwestern boy who dreams of working at a department store in the big city.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Joe Tapper, foreground, in “The Emporium” at the Classic Stage Company in Manhattan.

item.title: New York to Review Press Credentialing After Mangione Backers’ Comments
item.description: After some of Luigi Mangione’s supporters made comments that were condemned as inflammatory, Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s office said it would review the process that gave them press passes.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko and Anusha Bayya

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jeenah Moon
media:content.media:description: Some supporters of Luigi Mangione have been granted press credentials. On Monday, Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s office said it was evaluating how those passes are handed out after one supporter made comments that were quickly criticized.

item.title: La táctica de Trump sobre Taiwán ya es un regalo para China
item.description: La abierta disposición del presidente Trump a retrasar un paquete de armas para Taiwán por valor de 14.000 millones de dólares es una victoria para Pekín.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump con el líder chino, Xi Jinping, en Pekín, el jueves

item.title: Las deportaciones en EE. UU. han separado a más de 100.000 niños de sus padres
item.description: Tres cuartas partes de los niños separados de sus padres son probablemente ciudadanos estadounidenses, según estimaciones de la Institución Brookings que se compartieron con The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los niños han sido separados de sus padres, según un nuevo informe, mientras el gobierno de Trump continúa su impulso de deportación.

item.title: Southern California Fire Forces More Than 29,000 to Evacuate
item.description: Hundreds of firefighters battled a fast-moving brush fire that broke out in Simi Valley, Calif., on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Shatters a Neighborhood’s Peace
item.description: First came a series of pops, then a race to render aid. It was too late.
item.dc:creator: Candice Reed

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Women hug as they wait for the children to walk out of the mosque, at a collecting area, at the scene of a shooting at the Islamic Center in San Diego, California on Monday.

item.title: Dallas World Cup Mural: Artist Wyland Says Beloved Whale Artwork Was Painted Over
item.description: The artwork’s removal ahead of the World Cup has ignited a backlash over public art and what cities are willing to sacrifice for global events.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Julio Cortez/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The mural “Ocean Life” in downtown Dallas that is being painted over in preparation for the World Cup was dedicated in 1999.

item.title: Here’s How Iran Could Respond to Renewed U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: In any new round of fighting, Iranian officials could adopt new tactics, including intensified strikes on neighbors and trying to close off a second strait.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Iranians have used their proximity to the Strait of Hormuz, a critical trade waterway, to exert immense pressure on the global economy.

item.title: Mamdani, the ‘Tax-the-Rich’ Mayor, Reaches Out to Titans of Finance
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who has angered some corporate executives with his tax policies, has had a recent series of meetings with top business leaders.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Lauren Hirsch

item.title: U.S. Hits Top Cuban Officials With Sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration, ratcheting up its effort to cripple the Cuban government, also imposed sanctions on three government agencies, including the police.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:credit: Norlys Perez/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Cuban capital this week.

item.title: La aprobación de Trump alcanza un nuevo mínimo, según las encuestas
item.description: Un índice de aprobación del 37 por ciento en una nueva encuesta de Times/Siena sugiere que el Partido Republicano se enfrenta a un problema a mitad de legislatura.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El índice de aprobación del presidente Trump es el más bajo registrado en una encuesta Times/Siena.

item.title: The Generation That Grew Up With A.I. Hates It
item.description: Why graduates are booing artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Steven Senne/Associated Press

item.title: Officials Investigating San Diego Mosque Shooting as Possible Hate Crime
item.description: Three people were killed in an attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday. The authorities said a security guard who was fatally shot might have prevented a more deadly massacre.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Mike Blake/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Emergency workers responded to a shooting at the Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday.

item.title: Pollock and Brancusi Join the $100 Million Club at Auction
item.description: A “drip” painting by the Abstract Expressionist sold for $181.2 million with fees, while a bronze head by the Romanian sculptor brought in $107.6 million at Christie’s, both from the S.I. Newhouse collection.
item.dc:creator: Julia Halperin, Tim F. Schneider and Zachary Small

media:content.media:credit: Christie's
media:content.media:description: “Number 7A, 1948,” by Jackson Pollock, one of the treasures of S.I. Newhouse’s collection at Christie’s on Monday night, sold for $181.2 million with fees.

item.title: La identidad visual de Frida Kahlo
item.description: Corsés de yeso, vestidos de tehuana, trajes masculinos y monos araña: estos son los elementos que hicieron de Frida Kahlo un ícono irrepetible.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.category: El Ultimo Sueno de Frida y Diego (Opera)

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images; Ricky Rhodes for The New York Times

item.title: What to Know About San Diego’s Islamic Center
item.description: A visitor said that the mosque is highly diverse. Its services are in English, because attendees come from all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People embracing near the scene of a shooting outside the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

item.title: Latest Weather Updates: Tornadoes Reported in Kansas and Nebraska
item.description: Severe weather was expected to last into the evening on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff, Erin McCann and Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Jury Rejects Elon Musk’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI and Microsoft
item.description: Elon Musk had accused OpenAI of “stealing a charity” by attaching a commercial company to Open AI, which was founded as a nonprofit. But a jury ruled that the statute of limitations had expired.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif., on Monday, when a jury decided that Elon Musk had waited too long to file his lawsuit against OpenAI.

item.title: Why Josh Shapiro Isn’t Acting Like Other Potential 2028 Candidates
item.description: The Pennsylvania governor’s in-state focus is a contrast with other possible presidential hopefuls.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Gov. Josh Shapiro of Pennsylvania.

item.title: TrumpRx Adds Generic Drugs, With Mark Cuban, GoodRx and Amazon
item.description: President Trump announced the addition of 600 medicines to his online drugstore as he appeals to Americans concerned about high drug prices and affordability.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Mark Cuban during an event at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Nancy Pelosi Endorses Connie Chan to Succeed Her
item.description: Representative Nancy Pelosi, the first woman to serve as speaker of the House, broke her silence on Monday about the race to succeed her and endorsed Connie Chan, a San Francisco official.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nancy Pelosi at a dinner honoring her legacy at the state Democratic Party convention in San Francisco earlier this year.

item.title: Long Island Rail Road Strike Ends as Deal Is Reached
item.description: Workers for the nation’s busiest passenger rail service, who had not received a raise since 2022, were on strike for three days. Despite the agreement, commuting was likely to remain difficult on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
